core_mechanism: encoding a history-dependent recurrence as a generating/transform function that satisfies a first-order transport (advection-type) PDE, then extracting long-time asymptotics from the dominant singularity of that solution via singularity analysis, with a conformal (Schwarz-Christoffel) change of variables making the singularity structure explicit
brief_reason: The abstract turns the walk's Laplace-transform recurrence into a generating function governed by a transport-type PDE, solves it through a Schwarz-Christoffel mapping, and then applies singularity analysis to obtain the asymptotics.
